message:
perf(pager): use a monotonic eviction cursor in FlatPageSlots to spread eviction pressure across the table

Previously FlatPageSlots::remove_any_page() picked its starting probe
index by reading `count.load(Ordering::Relaxed) & mask`. Under
sequential scans (e.g. a full table scan filling a saturated buffer
pool) `count` quickly stabilizes near the high-water mark, so the
"random" starting index became almost-fixed and we kept rescanning
the same few slots before finding a victim.

Replaces that with an explicit `eviction_cursor: AtomicUsize` that
the eviction path increments on every call:

    let start = self.eviction_cursor.fetch_add(1, Ordering::Relaxed);

After a successful evict, the cursor is also nudged forward via
`eviction_cursor.fetch_add` past the just-freed slot so the next
caller starts probing further along instead of immediately re-trying
the same slot.

Effect: O(1) amortized victim probing across the full table for the
common sequential-scan-on-saturated-buffer-pool workload, rather than
O(table_size) wasted slot reads per eviction once the first probe
hot-spots on a non-evictable region.

Wraparound is handled by the existing `(start + i) & mask` indexing,
and the cursor itself is allowed to wrap on AtomicUsize overflow —
since the index is always masked, the wraparound is observationally
equivalent to a normal advance.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
